title: Zybahhod Yenyoni

The '''Zybahhod Yenyoni''' was a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] originating in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zybahhod system/Legends|Zybahhod system]]. They were well-adapted for life in [[Desert/Legends|deserts]], having special [[Salt/Legends|salt]] glands that removed [[Sodium/Legends|sodium]] from the brackish [[Water/Legends|water]] they [[Diet/Legends|drank]]. The Zybahhod Yenyoni played a [[Music/Legends|music]] instrument known as [[udjudg]] and, at some point, tribes of the species [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Zybahhod Yenyoni was a [[Human/Legends|Human]]-sized [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Having a slender build, their body had two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and two five-digited hands. Their head had five horn-like protrusions, two [[Color/Legends|yellow]], slitted [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a Y-shaped [[Nose/Legends|naso-facial]] opening, and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. The species also had a small tail with a tuft of [[Hair/Legends|hair]] at the end. The mottled and wrinkled skin of the Zybahhod Yenyoni was largely yellow and white, with individual strands of hair sprouting from all over the body and the [[Breast/Legends|breast]] having two pointed blue nipples.<ref name="Galaxy7" />

The semi-[[Local day/Legends|nocturnal]] Zybahhod Yenyoni were well adapted for life in [[Desert/Legends|deserts]], with their skin lacking sweat glands. They [[Diet/Legends|drank]] brackish [[Water/Legends|water]], from which special [[Salt/Legends|salt]] glands located in openings next to each eye removed [[Sodium/Legends|sodium]].<ref name="Galaxy7" />

==Society and culture==
The Zybahhod Yenyoni society was organized into tribes, with those residing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] retreating to underground [[Cave/Legends|caves]] during the most intense phases of [[Temperature/Legends|heat]] from its<ref name="Galaxy7" /> [[Binary star/Legends|twin suns]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Members of the species made [[Music/Legends|music]] by playing the instrument known as [[udjudg]] with their naso-facial openings. One Zybahhod Yenyoni who played the instrument carried one of their species' [[Youngling/Legends|young]] in a pouch on their back and [[Clothing/Legends|wore]] nothing but a pair of footwear that was made from a scaled [[Leather/Legends|leather]]–like material and had a sharp talon at the toe end of it.<ref name="Galaxy7" />

==Zybahhod Yenyoni in the galaxy==
The Zybahhod Yenyoni originated in<ref name="Planetnamia" /> the [[Zybahhod system/Legends|Zybahhod system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Herios sector/Legends|Herios sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> At some point, tribes of the species [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] on the planet Tatooine<ref name="Galaxy7" /> in the Outer Rim's [[Arkanis sector/Legends|Arkanis sector]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Zybahhod Yenyoni were created by [[Jennifer Kay]] through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition run in the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]''.<ref name="Galaxy7" /> The competition, launched in the magazine's [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|third issue]]<ref name="Galaxy3">{{GalaxyCite|3|Design An Alien|Design An Alien Contest}}</ref> in [[April]] [[1995]],<ref name="DateGalaxy3">{{GalaxyCite|2|Rebel Report}}</ref> called for readers to create completely new [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species inspired by the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' films.<ref name="Galaxy7" />

Kay was twenty-six years of age at the time of the Zybahhod Yenyoni entry's submission, which was later published in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seventh issue]] of the magazine<ref name="Galaxy7" /> in April [[1996]],<ref name="DateGalaxy7">{{GalaxyCite|6|Around the Galaxy}}</ref> coming in third in the "16 and up" subdivision of the competition.<ref name="Galaxy7" /> [[Lucasfilm]] employee [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], later stated that published material from the "Design An Alien" competition was considered [[canon]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Yes, though any text discrepencies [sic] with other sources would [sic] likely to superceded [sic] by the other source. {{C|in response to "Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon?"}}}}</ref>
